mission.title: keycloak: Step-up authentication for SAML clients
mission.description: Keycloak does not properly handle the SAML AuthnContextClassRef element in authentication requests from SAML clients. When a service provider requests a specific authentication context (e.g., MFA via https://refeds.org/profile/mfa), Keycloak returns an 'unspecified' value instead of honoring the requested context. Requested by 22+ users.

steps.title: Check current Keycloak setup
steps.description:
Verify your Keycloak version and SAML client configuration:
```bash
# Check Keycloak version:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config

# List SAML clients in the realm:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh get clients \
  -r myrealm --query protocol=saml --fields 'id,clientId'
```
This feature requires a Keycloak version that includes step-up authentication support for SAML.

steps.title: Understand the SAML step-up authentication requirement
steps.description:
SAML service providers can request specific authentication contexts via the `RequestedAuthnContext` element:
```xml
<samlp:RequestedAuthnContext>
  <saml:AuthnContextClassRef
    xmlns:saml="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:assertion">
    https://refeds.org/profile/mfa
  </saml:AuthnContextClassRef>
</samlp:RequestedAuthnContext>
```
Previously, Keycloak did not process this element for step-up authentication, always returning `urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:unspecified` regardless of the requested context.

steps.title: Upgrade to the fixed version
steps.description:
Upgrade your Keycloak installation to a version that includes SAML step-up authentication support from [PR #42984](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/42984):
```bash
docker pull quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest
docker stop keycloak && docker rm keycloak
docker run -d --name keycloak \
  -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin \
  -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:latest start-dev
```
After upgrading, configure your SAML client's authentication flow to map the requested AuthnContextClassRef to the appropriate authentication steps.

steps.title: Verify step-up authentication works for SAML clients
steps.description:
Test the feature:
1. Configure a SAML client that sends a `RequestedAuthnContext` with a specific `AuthnContextClassRef` (e.g., MFA)
2. Initiate a SAML login flow from the service provider
3. Verify that Keycloak triggers the appropriate step-up authentication (e.g., OTP prompt)
4. Check the SAML response contains the correct `AuthnContextClassRef` instead of `unspecified`

resolution.summary: Keycloak now supports step-up authentication for SAML clients by processing the RequestedAuthnContext element in SAML authentication requests. When a service provider requests a specific authentication context (e.g., MFA), Keycloak triggers the appropriate authentication flow and returns the correct AuthnContextClassRef in the SAML response.

prerequisites.description: A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes) with SAML client configuration access.
